---
title: "projectcode2"
author: "Owen Larson"
date: "4/17/2026"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
cat("data link: https://www.kaggle.com/datasets/jtrotman/formula-1-pitstops-1994-2010, time must be char")
```

```{r}
library(car)
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(readr)
library(corrplot)
```


```{r dataClean, timeParse}
str(pitstops)
names(pitstops)
head(pitstops)
# Keep raw copies
pitstops$Time_raw <- pitstops$Time
pitstops$Total_raw <- pitstops$Total

# Parse numeric time columns
pitstops$Time <- as.numeric(pitstops$Time_raw)
pitstops$Total_sec <- sapply(as.character(pitstops$Total_raw), function(val) {
  val <- trimws(val)
  if (is.na(val) || val == "") return(NA_real_)
  if (grepl(":", val)) {
    parts <- strsplit(val, ":")[[1]]
    return(as.numeric(parts[1]) * 60 + as.numeric(parts[2]))
  } else {
    return(as.numeric(val))
  }
})

# Recover missing Time from Total
pitstops <- pitstops %>%
  arrange(RaceId, DriverId, Stops) %>%
  group_by(RaceId, DriverId) %>%
  mutate(
    prev_total = lag(Total_sec),
    Time = case_when(
      !is.na(Time) ~ Time,
      is.na(Time) & Stops == 1 & !is.na(Total_sec) ~ Total_sec,
      is.na(Time) & Stops > 1 & !is.na(Total_sec) & !is.na(prev_total) ~ Total_sec - prev_total,
      TRUE ~ Time
    )
  ) %>%
  ungroup()

# Keep only the columns you actually want after reconstruction
pitstops <- pitstops %>%
  select(-Time_raw, -Total_raw, -Total_sec, -prev_total)

# Final check
sum(is.na(pitstops$Time))
str(pitstops)
summary(pitstops$Time)
```

```{r ogmodel, include=FALSE}
# save a copy 
pitstops_copy <- pitstops

# trim out absurd outliers (slowest 1%) and catastrophically long stops as well as one NA team
summary(pitstops_copy$Time)
quantile(pitstops_copy$Time, probs = c(0.90, 0.95, 0.99, 0.995, 0.999), na.rm = TRUE)

pitstops <- subset(pitstops, !is.na(Car) & trimws(Car) != "")

pitstops <- subset(pitstops, Time > 0 & Time <= 127)

# Create generation factor
pitstops$Generation <- 
  ifelse(pitstops$Year >= 1994 & pitstops$Year <= 2005, "Gen1_94_05", 
  ifelse(pitstops$Year >= 2006 & pitstops$Year <= 2010, "Gen2_06_10", NA))

pitstops$Generation <- factor(pitstops$Generation)

# Subset relevant data
pitstops_gen <- subset(pitstops, !is.na(Generation))

# Fit two-way ANOVA with interaction
model <- lm(Time ~ Car * Generation, data = pitstops_gen)

# Type II SS (recommended for your setup)
Anova(model, type = 2)

par(mfrow = c(2,2))
plot(model)
```
```{r logModel}
# Rebuild generation dataset from cleaned pitstops
pitstops$Generation <- 
  ifelse(pitstops$Year >= 1994 & pitstops$Year <= 2005, "Gen1_94_05",
  ifelse(pitstops$Year >= 2006 & pitstops$Year <= 2010, "Gen2_06_10", NA))

pitstops$Generation <- factor(pitstops$Generation)

pitstops_gen <- subset(pitstops, !is.na(Generation))
pitstops_gen$Car <- droplevels(factor(pitstops_gen$Car))
pitstops_gen$Generation <- droplevels(factor(pitstops_gen$Generation))

# Final filtered / logged dataset
pitstops_log <- subset(pitstops_gen,
                       complete.cases(Time, Car, Generation) &
                       is.finite(Time) &
                       Time > 0 &
                       Time <= 127)

pitstops_log$Car <- droplevels(factor(pitstops_log$Car))
pitstops_log$Generation <- droplevels(factor(pitstops_log$Generation))
pitstops_log$logTime <- log(pitstops_log$Time)

# original log Model
model_log <- lm(logTime ~ Car * Generation, data = pitstops_log)

Anova(model_log, type = 2)

# Main model + Race
model_race_full <- lm(logTime ~ Car * Generation + Race, data = pitstops_log)

Anova(model_race_full, type = 2)
par(mfrow = c(2,2))
plot(model_race_full)
```

```{r splitGen-TukeyHSD}
# --- Split data by generation ---
gen1 <- subset(pitstops_gen, Generation == "Gen1_94_05")
gen2 <- subset(pitstops_gen, Generation == "Gen2_06_10")

# --- ANOVA within each generation ---
model_gen1 <- aov(Time ~ Car + Race, data = gen1)
model_gen2 <- aov(Time ~ Car + Race, data = gen2)

summary(model_gen1)
summary(model_gen2)

# --- Tukey post-hoc comparisons ---
# Gen1 Tukey
tukey_gen1 <- as.data.frame(TukeyHSD(model_gen1)$Car)
tukey_gen1$Comparison <- rownames(tukey_gen1)
colnames(tukey_gen1) <- c("diff", "lower", "upper", "p_adj", "Comparison")
tukey_gen1 <- tukey_gen1[, c("Comparison", "diff", "lower", "upper", "p_adj")]
tukey_gen1 <- separate(tukey_gen1, Comparison, into = c("Team1", "Team2"), sep = "-")
tukey_gen1$abs_diff <- abs(tukey_gen1$diff)

sig_gen1 <- subset(tukey_gen1, p_adj < 0.05)

# Gen2 Tukey
tukey_gen2 <- as.data.frame(TukeyHSD(model_gen2)$Car)
tukey_gen2$Comparison <- rownames(tukey_gen2)
colnames(tukey_gen2) <- c("diff", "lower", "upper", "p_adj", "Comparison")
tukey_gen2 <- tukey_gen2[, c("Comparison", "diff", "lower", "upper", "p_adj")]
tukey_gen2 <- separate(tukey_gen2, Comparison, into = c("Team1", "Team2"), sep = "-")
tukey_gen2$abs_diff <- abs(tukey_gen2$diff)

sig_gen2 <- subset(tukey_gen2, p_adj < 0.05)

top_sig_gen1 <- head(sig_gen1[order(-sig_gen1$abs_diff), ], 10)
top_sig_gen2 <- head(sig_gen2[order(-sig_gen2$abs_diff), ], 10)
top_sig_gen1; top_sig_gen2

# --- Fastest teams (top 5) per generation ---
gen1_means <- aggregate(Time ~ Car, data = gen1, mean)
gen1_fastest <- gen1_means[order(gen1_means$Time), ][1:10, ]

gen2_means <- aggregate(Time ~ Car, data = gen2, mean)
gen2_fastest <- gen2_means[order(gen2_means$Time), ][1:10, ]

gen1_fastest; gen2_fastest
```

```{r btGenTop5_95CI}
# Gen1 summary for top 5 fastest teams with 95% CI
gen1_plot_data <- gen1 %>%
  filter(Car %in% gen1_fastest$Car) %>%
  group_by(Car) %>%
  summarise(
    n = n(),
    mean_time = mean(Time, na.rm = TRUE),
    sd_time = sd(Time, na.rm = TRUE),
    se_time = sd_time / sqrt(n),
    ci_time = qt(0.975, df = n - 1) * se_time,
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    se_time = ifelse(is.na(se_time), 0, se_time),
    ci_time = ifelse(is.na(ci_time), 0, ci_time)
  ) %>%
  arrange(mean_time)

# Gen2 summary for top 5 fastest teams with 95% CI
gen2_plot_data <- gen2 %>%
  filter(Car %in% gen2_fastest$Car) %>%
  group_by(Car) %>%
  summarise(
    n = n(),
    mean_time = mean(Time, na.rm = TRUE),
    sd_time = sd(Time, na.rm = TRUE),
    se_time = sd_time / sqrt(n),
    ci_time = qt(0.975, df = n - 1) * se_time,
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    se_time = ifelse(is.na(se_time), 0, se_time),
    ci_time = ifelse(is.na(ci_time), 0, ci_time)
  ) %>%
  arrange(mean_time)

p1 <- ggplot(gen1_plot_data, aes(x = reorder(Car, mean_time), y = mean_time)) +
  geom_point(size = 3, color = "blue") +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = mean_time - ci_time, ymax = mean_time + ci_time),
                width = 0.2, color = "blue") +
  coord_flip() +
  labs(title = "Gen1 (1994–2005): Top 5 Fastest Teams",
       x = "Team",
       y = "Mean Pit Stop Time (seconds) ± 95% CI") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    aspect.ratio = 0.35,
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 10),
    plot.margin = margin(5, 5, 5, 5)
  )

p2 <- ggplot(gen2_plot_data, aes(x = reorder(Car, mean_time), y = mean_time)) +
  geom_point(size = 3, color = "red") +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = mean_time - ci_time, ymax = mean_time + ci_time),
                width = 0.2, color = "red") +
  coord_flip() +
  labs(title = "Gen2 (2006–2010): Top 5 Fastest Teams",
       x = "Team",
       y = "Mean Pit Stop Time (seconds) ± 95% CI") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    aspect.ratio = 0.35,
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 10),
    plot.margin = margin(5, 5, 5, 5)
  )

p1; p2
```

```{r genPitStopTime}

# Mean pit stop time per team per generation
team_gen_means <- aggregate(Time ~ Car + Generation, data = pitstops_gen, mean)

# --- 2. TREND OVER YEARS (IMPORTANT PLOT) ---
year_means <- aggregate(Time ~ Year, data = pitstops_gen, mean)

plot(year_means$Year, year_means$Time,
     type = "b",
     pch = 19,
     lwd = 2,
     col = "blue",
     main = "Average Pit Stop Time Over Years",
     xlab = "Year",
     ylab = "Average Pit Stop Time")

# Add generation split line
abline(v = 2005.5, col = "red", lty = 2)

# --- 3. GENERATION COMPARISON ---

boxplot(Time ~ Generation, data = pitstops_gen,
        col = c("lightblue", "lightcoral"),
        main = "Pit Stop Time by Generation",
        ylab = "Time")
```

```{r pitStopTrendsPlot}
# --- Aggregate mean per Year × Car × Generation ---
agg <- aggregate(Time ~ Year + Car + Generation, data = pitstops_gen, mean)

# --- Find top 5 teams (by total observations, not just appearance in one year) ---
team_counts <- sort(table(pitstops_gen$Car), decreasing = TRUE)
top10 <- names(team_counts)[1:5]

# Subset aggregated data
plot_data <- subset(agg, Car %in% top5)
plot_data$Car <- droplevels(plot_data$Car)

# --- Plot ---
ggplot(plot_data, aes(x = factor(Year), y = Time, color = Car, group = Car)) +
  geom_line(size = 1) +
  geom_point() +
  facet_wrap(~ Generation, scales = "free_x") +
  labs(title = "Top 5 Teams: Pit Stop Trends by Generation",
       x = "Year",
       y = "Mean Pit Stop Time") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 90, vjust = 0.5))
```

```{r correlation}
# Keep only the most meaningful numeric variables for correlation
corr_vars <- pitstops_gen[, c("Time", "Year", "Lap", "Stops")]

# Summary
summary(corr_vars)

# Correlation matrix
cor_matrix <- cor(corr_vars, use = "pairwise.complete.obs", method = "pearson")
round(cor_matrix, 3)

# Pairwise correlation tests
cor_driver <- function(data) {
  vars <- names(data)
  results <- data.frame(
    Var1 = character(),
    Var2 = character(),
    Correlation = numeric(),
    p_value = numeric(),
    stringsAsFactors = FALSE
  )
  
  for (i in 1:(length(vars) - 1)) {
    for (j in (i + 1):length(vars)) {
      x <- data[[vars[i]]]
      y <- data[[vars[j]]]
      keep <- complete.cases(x, y)
      
      test <- cor.test(x[keep], y[keep], method = "pearson")
      
      results <- rbind(results, data.frame(
        Var1 = vars[i],
        Var2 = vars[j],
        Correlation = unname(test$estimate),
        p_value = test$p.value,
        stringsAsFactors = FALSE
      ))
    }
  }
  
  results[order(-abs(results$Correlation)), ]
}

cor_results <- cor_driver(corr_vars)
cor_results

# Visual correlation plot
corrplot(cor_matrix, method = "color", type = "upper",
         tl.col = "black", tl.srt = 45)

# Scatterplots against Time
par(mfrow = c(1, 3))

plot(pitstops_gen$Year, pitstops_gen$Time,
     xlab = "Year", ylab = "Pit Stop Time",
     main = "Time vs Year", pch = 19)
abline(lm(Time ~ Year, data = pitstops_gen), col = "red", lwd = 2)

plot(pitstops_gen$Lap, pitstops_gen$Time,
     xlab = "Lap", ylab = "Pit Stop Time",
     main = "Time vs Lap", pch = 19)
abline(lm(Time ~ Lap, data = pitstops_gen), col = "red", lwd = 2)

plot(pitstops_gen$Stops, pitstops_gen$Time,
     xlab = "Stops", ylab = "Pit Stop Time",
     main = "Time vs Stops", pch = 19)
abline(lm(Time ~ Stops, data = pitstops_gen), col = "red", lwd = 2)

par(mfrow = c(1, 1))

# -----------------------------
# Race as a categorical factor
# -----------------------------

# One-way ANOVA for Race
model_race <- aov(Time ~ Race, data = pitstops_gen)
summary(model_race)

# Mean pit stop time by Race
race_summary <- aggregate(Time ~ Race, data = pitstops_gen, mean)
race_summary <- race_summary[order(race_summary$Time), ]
race_summary

# Top 5 shortest and longest races
top_short_races <- head(race_summary, 5)
top_long_races  <- tail(race_summary, 5)
extreme_races   <- rbind(top_short_races, top_long_races)

extreme_races

# Plot shortest and longest races
ggplot(extreme_races, aes(x = reorder(Race, Time), y = Time)) +
  geom_col() +
  coord_flip() +
  labs(title = "Races with the Shortest and Longest Mean Pit Stop Times",
       x = "Race",
       y = "Mean Pit Stop Time (seconds)") +
  theme_minimal()
```

```{r 2010 mean time check, include=FALSE}
# 2010 only
pit2010 <- subset(pitstops_gen, Year == 2010)

# Summary stats
summary(pit2010$Time)
mean(pit2010$Time, na.rm = TRUE)
median(pit2010$Time, na.rm = TRUE)
quantile(pit2010$Time, probs = c(0.1, 0.25, 0.5, 0.75, 0.9, 0.95, 0.99), na.rm = TRUE)

# If you want trimmed 2010 too
pit2010_trim <- subset(pitstops_gen, Year == 2010 & Time <= 300)

summary(pit2010_trim$Time)
mean(pit2010_trim$Time, na.rm = TRUE)
median(pit2010_trim$Time, na.rm = TRUE)
quantile(pit2010_trim$Time, probs = c(0.1, 0.25, 0.5, 0.75, 0.9, 0.95, 0.99), na.rm = TRUE)

# Histogram
hist(pit2010_trim$Time,
     breaks = 30,
     main = "2010 Pit Stop Time Distribution",
     xlab = "Pit Stop Time")

# Fastest average teams in 2010
team2010 <- aggregate(Time ~ Car, data = pit2010_trim, mean)
team2010[order(team2010$Time), ][1:10, ]
```

```{r racePlots}
# Use the final filtered dataset
race_data <- pitstops_gen

# -----------------------------
# 1. Mean pit stop time by race
# -----------------------------
race_summary <- race_data %>%
  group_by(Race) %>%
  summarise(
    n = n(),
    mean_time = mean(Time, na.rm = TRUE),
    sd_time = sd(Time, na.rm = TRUE),
    se_time = sd_time / sqrt(n),
    ci_time = qt(0.975, df = n - 1) * se_time,
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  arrange(mean_time)

# Full race means table
race_summary

# -----------------------------------------
# 2. Top 5 shortest and top 5 longest races
# -----------------------------------------
top_short_races <- head(race_summary, 5)
top_long_races  <- tail(race_summary, 5)
extreme_races   <- bind_rows(top_short_races, top_long_races)

extreme_races

# Bar plot for shortest/longest races
p_race_bar <- ggplot(extreme_races, aes(x = reorder(Race, mean_time), y = mean_time)) +
  geom_col() +
  coord_flip() +
  labs(title = "Races with the Shortest and Longest Mean Pit Stop Times",
       x = "Race",
       y = "Mean Pit Stop Time (seconds)") +
  theme_minimal()

p_race_bar

# Error bar plot for shortest/longest races
p_race_error <- ggplot(extreme_races, aes(x = reorder(Race, mean_time), y = mean_time)) +
  geom_point(size = 3) +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = mean_time - ci_time, ymax = mean_time + ci_time), width = 0.2) +
  coord_flip() +
  labs(title = "Shortest and Longest Mean Pit Stop Times by Race",
       x = "Race",
       y = "Mean Pit Stop Time (seconds) ± 95% CI") +
  theme_minimal()

p_race_error

# -----------------------------
# 3. Boxplot by race
# -----------------------------
# Top 10 races by frequency only, so it stays readable
top_races_freq <- race_data %>%
  count(Race, sort = TRUE) %>%
  slice(1:10) %>%
  pull(Race)

race_box_data <- subset(race_data, Race %in% top_races_freq)

p_race_box <- ggplot(race_box_data, aes(x = reorder(Race, Time, FUN = median), y = Time)) +
  geom_boxplot() +
  coord_flip() +
  labs(title = "Pit Stop Time Distribution for the 10 Most Frequent Races",
       x = "Race",
       y = "Pit Stop Time (seconds)") +
  theme_minimal()

p_race_box

# ----------------------------------------
# 4. Mean pit stop time by race and generation
# ----------------------------------------
race_gen_summary <- race_data %>%
  group_by(Race, Generation) %>%
  summarise(
    n = n(),
    mean_time = mean(Time, na.rm = TRUE),
    sd_time = sd(Time, na.rm = TRUE),
    se_time = sd_time / sqrt(n),
    ci_time = qt(0.975, df = n - 1) * se_time,
    .groups = "drop"
  )

race_gen_summary

# Plot all race means split by generation
p_race_gen <- ggplot(race_gen_summary, aes(x = reorder(Race, mean_time), y = mean_time, fill = Generation)) +
  geom_col(position = "dodge") +
  coord_flip() +
  labs(title = "Mean Pit Stop Time by Race and Generation",
       x = "Race",
       y = "Mean Pit Stop Time (seconds)") +
  theme_minimal()

p_race_gen

# --------------------------------------------------
# 5. Only races appearing in both generations
# --------------------------------------------------
overlap_races <- race_data %>%
  group_by(Race) %>%
  summarise(n_gen = n_distinct(Generation), .groups = "drop") %>%
  filter(n_gen == 2) %>%
  pull(Race)

race_overlap_data <- race_gen_summary %>%
  filter(Race %in% overlap_races)

p_race_overlap <- ggplot(race_overlap_data,
                         aes(x = Generation, y = mean_time, group = Race, color = Race)) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 1) +
  geom_point(size = 3) +
  labs(title = "Mean Pit Stop Time by Generation for Races Appearing in Both Eras",
       x = "Generation",
       y = "Mean Pit Stop Time (seconds)",
       color = "Race") +
  theme_minimal()

p_race_overlap

# --------------------------------------------------
# 6. Heatmap of mean pit stop time by race and generation
# --------------------------------------------------
p_race_heat <- ggplot(race_gen_summary, aes(x = Generation, y = Race, fill = mean_time)) +
  geom_tile() +
  labs(title = "Heatmap of Mean Pit Stop Time by Race and Generation",
       x = "Generation",
       y = "Race",
       fill = "Mean Time") +
  theme_minimal()

p_race_heat
```
